finding.message: This expression logs [sensitive data (private)](1) as clear text.

verification.reasoning: The code directly prints sensitive CVV data in cleartext to stdout on line 17 without any encryption or protection, matching the rule's description of clear text logging of sensitive data
verification.data_flow: cvv generation (line 14) → cvv variable → print output (line 17)
answers: The sensitive data is CVV (Card Verification Value) payment card information, visible on line 17 where cvv.decode() is printed
The data is stored in console output via print() function on line 17
No, the data is not encrypted or hashed before output - it's printed in cleartext
Yes, an attacker with access to console logs or output captures could read the CVV data
The storage is reachable whenever this script runs, though it appears to be a utility script
High blast radius - CVV exposure could enable financial fraud and regulatory violations
